data.title: As a code reviewer agent, I emit findings and summary without scores or REVIEW_RESULT
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update all reviewer agent definitions and shared templates to emit the new two-key schema (findings + summary) and the reduced 3-line output contract (REVIEWER_HASH, FINDING_COUNT, FILES). Add mandatory pre-output checklist to preserve dimensional coverage.
**Why**: Reviewer agents are the producers of review output — they must emit the format that the updated infrastructure (Story 1) expects.
**Scope**:
- IN: reviewer-base.md (remove scores schema, REVIEW_RESULT, MIN_SCORE from output contract, add pre-output category checklist), all 6 reviewer delta files (remove N/A score instructions, add explicit scope negative constraints), all 11 agent definition files including 4 overlay agents (code-reviewer-light, standard, deep-correctness, deep-verification, deep-hygiene, deep-arch, security-red-team, security-blue-team, performance, test-quality), code-review-dispatch.md (remove scores and REVIEW_RESULT references)
- OUT: Infrastructure scripts (Story 1), workflow orchestration (Story 3), documentation (Story 4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, no reviewer agent definition file contains references to REVIEW_RESULT, MIN_SCORE, or a scores object in its output contract
  ← Satisfies: "The reviewer agent output contract no longer includes REVIEW_RESULT or MIN_SCORE lines"
- When this story is complete, reviewer-base.md specifies exactly two top-level keys (findings, summary) in the JSON schema and a 3-line output format (REVIEWER_HASH, FINDING_COUNT, FILES)
  ← Satisfies: "The reviewer-findings.json schema requires exactly two top-level keys: findings and summary"
- When this story is complete, each reviewer agent definition includes a mandatory pre-output checklist requiring consideration of all 5 categories (correctness, verification, hygiene, design, maintainability) before writing findings
  ← Satisfies: Prompt alignment mitigation for dimensional coverage collapse
- When this story is complete, deep-tier specialist delta files include explicit negative constraints ("Emit findings only in your owned category")
  ← Satisfies: Prompt alignment mitigation for specialist scope drift
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] reviewer-base.md is the shared template — changes propagate to all agents, but 6 delta files need individual attention
- [Reliability] All 4 overlay agents (security-red-team, security-blue-team, performance, test-quality) must be included — scenario analysis flagged this as critical

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
